read.thesis: The lecture argues that the Iran war is not only a state conflict. It is a fight over the social hallucination that makes the American-led order feel real. The global economy extracts consciousness through the dollar, price hierarchy, education, media, culture, science, and consumerism. That host system is then fed on by a parasite class: transnational capital, families, secret societies, intelligence, crime, and academic legitimation. Jeffrey Epstein matters because he is presented as an operator of that parasite system, not a marginal scandal. The Epstein files are public, Jiang says, because the parasite is no longer hidden from rival elites. What looks like transparency is an elite civil war over who gets to feed next.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: Start with the cave, not Epstein. The lecture begins by asking why the United States-Iran war started, how it will end, and what comes after. The answer has to pass through Plato because Jiang's first claim is that reality is a collective fantasy. People stare at shadows, name them, build language around them, and then defend the fantasy as if it were the real world. Wealth is the same kind of trick. It is conscious attention stored in things, and capital is the mechanism that extracts and warehouses that attention. Religion, Homer, the Bible, the dollar, and artificial intelligence are all technologies for focusing consciousness. The Iran war therefore becomes a war over the operating reality itself: the old dollar-world, the new AI-world, the imperial story that keeps everyone playing, and the parasite network that has learned to monetize the game.
